title: Convert a decimal string to Money
scenario: Forms pass prices as decimal strings such as "19.99". To avoid float rounding bugs, parse those strings into integer minor units with explicit errors and checked arithmetic.
instructions: Implement TryFrom<&str> for Money. Assume the parsed amount is USD. Return specific MoneyParseError variants for empty input, invalid digits, too many decimal places, and overflow.

hints.body: Split once on the decimal point so the major and minor parts can be validated separately.
Parse digits into u64, use checked_mul and checked_add, and map each expected failure to a MoneyParseError variant.
A canonical solution parses text without floats and preserves every expected failure as a typed error.

completionExplanation: Parsing money through TryFrom gives callers a standard fallible conversion API. Using integer minor units and checked arithmetic avoids floating-point rounding, overflow surprises, and panics.
